fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 39-year-old Asian American female with schizoaffective disorder here for medication review.
fields.Subjective: Ms. Patel presented for scheduled visit appearing well and in good spirits. Reports consistent medication adherence, taking asenapine 10 mg sublingual twice daily as directed. She uses a pill timer with alarms that has proven very effective for her. States she is doing quite well overall with only occasional mild symptoms. Experiences very infrequent auditory hallucinations - perhaps once every two weeks - of soft music playing that she recognizes as not real. No paranoid thoughts or other delusional content. Sleep pattern is regular at 6-7 hours per night, feels rested upon waking. Appetite good, weight maintained at 138 lbs. She returned to her position as a librarian at the public library six months ago working 25 hours per week and receiving positive feedback from supervisor. Mood is 'pretty good most days' rated 7-8/10. She and her husband enjoy weekly date nights and recently adopted a rescue dog they both adore. Continues individual therapy biweekly which she finds helpful for coping skills. Denies substance use. No safety concerns whatsoever, explicitly denies SI/HI. (Seen by Veronica Santos, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Professionally dressed, excellent grooming, pleasant demeanor
Mental_Status_Examination.Speech: Fluent, clear, normal pace and prosody
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Minimal residual hallucinations with good insight, no delusions

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: Routine labs all within normal limits including CBC, CMP, lipids, and HbA1c.

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizoaffective Disorder, stable with minimal residual symptoms.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Married; working part-time as librarian; engaged in therapy; strong support system; good functional recovery.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent adherence to asenapine with automated reminder system.
Symptoms well-controlled with good functional status.
Return to clinic in 8 weeks for routine follow-up.
fields.Author: Note written by Veronica Santos, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner — Bayside Mental Health, 567 Ocean Avenue, Santa Monica, CA 90401, Phone: (555) 821-7634, Fax: (555) 821-7635
